topic: HTTP client request bar: color-coded method dropdown, variable-highlighting URL input with unknown-var flagging against active env, and Send/Save/Share actions with keyboard shortcuts; reads/writes active tab RequestSpec
verbatim_prompt: The request bar of an HTTP client: a method dropdown (GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/OPTIONS/HEAD, color-coded by method), a URL input that highlights {{variable}} tokens and flags unknown ones against the active environment's valid-vars set, and Send / Save / Share actions. Send is blocked when the URL is empty and triggers the send flow; ⌘↵ sends, ⌘S saves. Reads and writes the active request tab's RequestSpec (method + url) already present in the renderer's tab store. Use the existing Dropdown molecule from specs/001-ui-primitives (Radix-backed, controlled via open/onOpenChange) for the method selector rather than a new dropdown. HTTP execution and the response are separate tasks. Visual reference: design/reference.html — look and behavior only, never reproduce its markup; it carries generated cruft (data-om-* attributes, __OmT wrappers, inline styles, a tweaks-panel) that must not enter the build. Rebuild with semantic class names from each component's role and its tag's function. Colors come from design/tokens.json.

functional_scope.value: RequestBar UI for the active request tab. (1) Method selector: dropdown over GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/OPTIONS/HEAD, each method color-coded via the 005 method-pill [data-mstyle] convention; reuses the 001 Dropdown molecule. (2) URL field: plain text input bound to RequestSpec.url (no var-highlight editor — that is epic D). (3) Actions: Send (disabled when url empty; fires a send-intent callback, NO HTTP here), Save (writes via the 004 dirty-flag + markClean path), Share (rendered token-styled in final layout position but wired to disabled/no-op stub — real share deferred). (4) Keyboard: ⌘↵ = Send, ⌘S = Save. Reads/writes only method+url of the active tab RequestSpec; does not touch auth.

users.value: Single local developer/API-tester operating the mintEnvoy desktop renderer. Keyboard-driven (⌘↵ Send, ⌘S Save), mouse for method selection + Share. No multi-user, no auth/identity, no remote/collab surface.

inputs_outputs.value: In: reactive read — subscribe to the active tab's RequestSpec so external/tab-switch changes re-render the bar; active-tab identity (which tab to read/write) is also an input; user edits (url text, method choice); action triggers (Send/Save/Share clicks, ⌘↵/⌘S). Out: writes (method+url) target the active tab only via 004 dirty-flag (set on change, cleared by Save->markClean); no write on pure focus/blur or method-menu open without a change. Send-intent emitted only when url is non-empty-after-trim, carrying nothing beyond the active tab's identity / current RequestSpec (method+url); no HTTP, no response data, no disk I/O. Share emits nothing (no-op stub).

integration_points.value: BELIEF (Phase 2/CBM reconciles actual paths + exports): RequestBar = request-domain organism under organisms/request/ (006 domain-nesting rule; same domain home as TabBar, NOT flat organisms/ root) — confirm the request domain folder exists post-006 and follow TabBar if it moved there. Consumes the existing Dropdown molecule + Icon atom (001) — bind real module paths via CBM, not assumed filenames. Reads/writes the active tab's RequestSpec through the existing tab store + RequestSpec model/state-machine (004); dirty + markClean are that module's API — reference by role, CBM resolves actual store/model files + exported symbols. Method pill color via 005 [data-mstyle] convention. Mounted by the shell/layout organism hosting the request pane (003 app-shell region) — confirm exact mount point + how active-tab context reaches it. @renderer alias; no sibling-organism imports (constitution §2.2).

constraints.value: tokens.css semantic classes, zero inline styles; no design-export JSX/cruft (data-om-*, __OmT wrappers, tweaks-panel). Reuse 001 Dropdown (Radix) for method selector — no second overlay lib; preserve its a11y; icons from existing Icon atom. Method color via 005 [data-mstyle] convention (consume, don't reinvent). Writes only via 004 dirty-flag + markClean; do not touch auth. Renderer process only; React 19 + TS strict; semantic class names from role/tag function. @renderer alias for imports; new files per atomic structure (RequestBar is an organism in its domain home per 006 placement rule); no sibling-organism imports. Co-located tests under __tests__/ (.test.tsx Vitest + .ct.tsx Playwright CT) matching established convention. Shortcut registration must not leak across tabs/unmount: bind to active tab, clean up listeners; ⌘S must not trigger browser/native save (preventDefault). No new runtime deps (Dropdown/Icon/state all exist); no network, no disk, no telemetry. Single-line URL input only (no textarea/multiline); horizontal scroll, no reflow. a11y: method selector keyboard-operable via Radix Dropdown semantics; Send disabled-state accessible (aria-disabled / disabled).

non_goals.value: HTTP execution / actually sending the request (separate task). Response rendering (separate task). {{variable}} highlighting + unknown-var flagging + interpolation -> epic D. No inline-highlight editor (CodeMirror/contenteditable overlay). Auth editing (discriminated-union placeholder, later task). Real Share behavior (cURL/link/export). No new dropdown/overlay library. URL parsing/validation/autocomplete/history-suggestions — url is a plain bound string (no scheme-check, no past-url dropdown). Query-param/KV editor (separate task). Body, headers, request sub-tabs (separate tasks). Durable persistence to disk/collections — Save persists the in-memory RequestSpec via 004 dirty/markClean only; persistence-port is its own task. Tab create/close/reorder/rename — owned by existing tab strip (002); this feature only reads/writes the focused tab's RequestSpec. Send-intent callback consumer (HTTP engine) is out — this feature fires the intent only.

success_criteria.value: Layout [method ▾][URL][Send] matches Postman/Insomnia pattern + design reference visually; semantic classes bound to tokens.css, zero inline styles; Share button in final slot disabled/no-op. RequestSpec read: bar renders active tab method+url; tab switch swaps values with no cross-tab bleed. RequestSpec write: url edit + method change write back to active tab only; method pill uses 005 [data-mstyle]. Dirty integration: any method/url edit marks tab dirty via 004; Save (when dirty) persists + markClean clears flag; Save on non-dirty = no-op. Send: disabled when url empty-after-trim, else fires send-intent callback (no HTTP). Shortcuts: ⌘↵ Send + ⌘S Save act on active tab globally; Save respects dirty; trim guard applies to ⌘↵. Overflow: long url scrolls horizontally, method pill+Send do not reflow. Method selector reuses 001 Dropdown (no second overlay lib); icons use existing Icon atom. Tests green: unit (RequestSpec read/write, trim guard, dirty/markClean, method-switch independence) + CT (layout, disabled Send, shortcut behavior, per-tab isolation); typecheck+lint+build pass.

edge_cases.value: Whitespace-only URL: trim before the empty-guard; empty-after-trim blocks Send (same disabled state as empty); never send a whitespace URL. ⌘S on a non-dirty tab: no-op (no write, no markClean, no toast) — Save acts only when dirty. Shortcut focus scope: ⌘↵ (Send) and ⌘S (Save) act on the active tab globally regardless of focus (target the active tab RequestSpec); URL-edit shortcuts stay local to the input. Rapid method switching mid-edit: method and url are independent RequestSpec fields — switching method never touches the url buffer or caret; each method change is its own committed dirty edit, no debounce coupling. Very long URL: single-line input, no wrap, horizontal scroll within the field; layout (method pill/Send) must not reflow or grow; no truncation of stored value, visual scroll only.

references: Postman/Insomnia request-bar layout ([method ▾][URL][Send]) — visual reference only, build from scratch
Dropdown molecule + Icon atom (specs/001-ui-primitives) — reuse Radix Dropdown for method selector, no second overlay lib
RequestSpec / working-tabs state machine (specs/004-working-tabs-state-machine) — method+url are RequestSpec fields, writes via dirty-flag + markClean
method-pill color convention (specs/005-tab-bar-visual-fidelity) — runtime [data-mstyle] attribute, consume it
tokens.css semantic classes, zero inline styles

gaps.description: user-supplied placement guess (confirm via Phase 2 fit-check): active request tab RequestSpec (method+url) already present in renderer tab store — request bar reads/writes it

intake_classifications.statement: Request bar UI: color-coded method dropdown (GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/OPTIONS/HEAD), URL input that highlights {{variable}} tokens and flags unknown ones against active env valid-vars, and Send/Save/Share actions (Send blocked on empty URL, triggers send flow; ⌘↵ sends, ⌘S saves), reading/writing the active tab RequestSpec (method+url). HTTP execution + response are separate tasks.
intake_classifications.minimal_fix: Single RequestBar component bound to the active tab's RequestSpec (method+url) in the renderer tab store: method <select> styled per-method color, controlled URL <input> with token-highlight overlay + unknown-var flag against the active environment, Send/Save/Share buttons (Send disabled when url empty; Send fires a send-intent callback, ⌘↵=Send, ⌘S=Save). No HTTP, no response rendering.

intake_classifications.statement: Use the existing Dropdown molecule from specs/001-ui-primitives (Radix-backed, open/onOpenChange) for the method selector rather than a new dropdown
Active request tab RequestSpec (method+url) is already present in the renderer tab store; the request bar reads/writes it
